sections.paragraph:
fut. יַחֲלֹם-

(1) Arabic حلَمَ Conj. I. V. to be fat, fleshy, spoken of an infant, flocks, see the Arabic lexicographers in Scheid, Cant. Hiskiæ, page 140 (cogn. חָלַב, حلس). Hence once Job 39:4, to become strong or robust (Syr. Pe. and Ethpe. to become sound or strong).

(2) to dream (because, it is said, fatness of body inclines to sleep and dreams; at all events the significations of fatness and dreaming are often found in the other cognate languages expressed by the same letters. Arab. حلم Eth. ሐለመ፡ Syr. ܚܠܰܡ), Gen 37:5, seq.; 42:9 Isa 29:8. חֹלֵם חֲלֹום a dreamer of dreams, i.q. נָבִיא, inasmuch as dreams were ascribed to divine inspiration [or rather because revelations were often made to God’s true prophets in dreams], Deu 13:2, Deu 13:4compare Joe 3:1 Num 12:6.

Hiphil-

(1) to cause to recover, Isa 38:16.-(2) to cause to dream, Jer 29:8.

Derivatives, חֲלֹום, חַלָּמוּת, אַחְלְמָה [also חֵלֶם and patron. נֶחֱלָמִי].
